title: Touch targets must meet minimum size requirements
summary: AI-generated mobile interfaces often have touch targets smaller than 44x44px, causing user frustration on touch devices.
instruction: On mobile interfaces, ensure every interactive element has a rendered touch target of at least 44x44 CSS pixels. Use padding to extend the hit area beyond the visible element. This is an Apple HIG recommendation for comfortable touch interaction.
rationale: Apple HIG recommends a minimum 44x44pt touch target for comfortable interaction across iOS device sizes. Users with larger fingers, reduced dexterity, or using the device one-handed need generous tap areas. Small targets increase error rates disproportionately. This rule applies the Apple guideline as a mobile-specific design standard.

violations: A mobile navigation with 24px-tall links and 4px gaps between them.
Icon buttons (edit, delete, share) at 28x28px without padding extending the hit area.
controls: Mobile navigation links with 48px height and 8px gap between interactive rows.
Icon buttons at 24x24px visible size with total 44x44px touch target through transparent padding.
borderline: Interactive elements in a scrollable data table where 44px row height reduces data density - needs documented tradeoff.

exceptions.description: Desktop-only interfaces where touch is not the primary input. Elements inside scrollable containers where the parent handles touch. Inline text links within paragraphs.

detector.signalDescription: For interactive elements (a, button, input, select, [role=button]), compute the rendered box-model touch area including padding and border. Flag if the total area is smaller than 44x44 CSS pixels at mobile viewport breakpoints.
limitations: Static CSS analysis can approximate dimensions but only the rendered box model gives the actual touch target size.
JavaScript-determined touch areas and custom gesture handlers may not appear in static source.
Responsive elements that resize at different breakpoints must be evaluated per breakpoint.
Overlapping elements may have effective touch areas differing from their box-model dimensions.

scenarios: Desktop-only interfaces where touch is not the primary input.
Inline text links where 44px would break paragraph flow.
mitigations: Apply only at mobile viewport breakpoints (<768px). Exclude inline text links.

fixturePlan.description: A mobile form with 32px submit button, 28px icon buttons, 24px navigation links.
Same page with 48px submit button, 44px icon buttons via padding, 44px nav links.
A mobile data table with 36px row heights but 44px touch-friendly checkboxes leading each row.

notes: Normalization correction: scoped to mobile surfaces and Apple HIG only. Removed Material Design reference (no source record). Changed detector from static CSS-property to rendered-box measurement. Maintainer review.
